meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in tutorials. Learn how to master project management for AI SaaS, using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster than ever.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of aimless coding. To escape, you must shift from passive consumption to aggressive project management. By defining a strict MVP scope, utilizing Antigravity for architectural scaffolding, and leveraging Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform abstract tutorials into a scalable, revenue-generating SaaS product.
content.intro:
You have spent months watching YouTube tutorials, collecting boilerplate code, and tweaking CSS, yet your product remains a ghost in a local directory. You are trapped in the 'Tutorial Hell' cycle—a seductive trap where the dopamine of learning replaces the grit of execution. Your code is clean, but your bank account is empty because you aren't building a product; you are building a repository of unfinished dreams.

Real founders don't learn by watching; they learn by breaking things in production. The shift happens when you stop viewing coding as a craft and start viewing it as a project management challenge. By integrating high-leverage tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate the drudgery and force yourself into the only metric that matters: speed-to-market.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Stop building features. Define one singular problem your user will pay to solve. Scope the project to a 7-day development cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Given my goal to build [PROJECT NAME], break this down into an atomic MVP scope that can be shipped in 7 days, focusing only on the core value proposition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a project roadmap file named MVP_SCOPE.md detailing the core features required for a minimum viable product launch.'

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a production-ready folder structure. Avoid the 'blank file' paralysis by generating your stack boilerplate immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade directory structure for a Next.js 14 SaaS application using TypeScript, Tailwind, and Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate the file structure and initial configuration files based on the project roadmap.'

steps.title: Database Schema Modeling
steps.description: Your database is the heart of your SaaS. Use Claude Code to define your schema before writing a single line of frontend code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS platform with user authentication, subscription tiers, and a project dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create the schema.prisma file and run migration commands based on the defined database requirements.'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Implementation
steps.description: Execute your project plan by feeding small, scoped tasks into Claude Code to ensure the code stays modular and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, reusable React component for a subscription pricing table that integrates with Stripe.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Implement the pricing component and add it to the landing page route.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Gates
steps.description: Avoid tech debt by forcing Claude Code to write tests for every feature implemented. No test, no commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Playwright tests for the user authentication flow, ensuring edge cases are covered.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate unit tests for all new functions and ensure they pass before final commit.'

pro_tips: Treat your IDE like a terminal; keep the UI hidden and focus on CLI-driven development to increase velocity.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, you are likely over-engineering; cut the scope or use an API abstraction.
Always commit after every single functional unit is verified; this creates a 'save point' if your AI-generated code hallucinates.
Use Claude Code to document your code as it is written; documentation is a feature, not an afterthought.
Prioritize billing and auth first; if you can't collect money, you aren't running a business, you're running a hobby.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is functional, deploy to Vercel and immediately initiate your first cold outreach campaign to validate the business model.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the professional-grade workflow. The only thing missing is your execution. Stop theorizing and start shipping the product that changes your trajectory. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
